business.headline: Do you own too many mutual funds? How overdiversification can hurt returnsAdding a new mutual fund every year feels like a smart move. But at what point does diversification turn into overdiversification and start hurting returns? Let's find out.
Do stock market returns really beat FDs after taxes and risk?Ace investor Shankar Sharma has questioned whether stock markets really outperform fixed deposits once taxes and volatility are counted.

Will AI reduce hiring? TCS Chairman explains what lies ahead for IT jobsSpeaking at TCS's 31st annual general meeting, N Chandrasekaran outlined a future where AI agents become an integral part of the workforce.
Equity mutual fund inflows drop 40.4% month-on-month amid global uncertaintyAMFI data showed equity mutual fund inflows fell 40.4% to Rs 22,907.77 crore in May from Rs 38,440.20 crore in April.

Gold falls below Rs 1.5 lakh, silver drops Rs 4,000. Here's whyGold and silver slumped in India after heavy selling in global bullion markets. A stronger dollar, rising oil prices and revived rate hike fears deepened pressure on precious metals.

CleanMax shares jump 15% to record high: Why the stock is rising todayCleanMax and Meta have partnered to develop more than 900 MW of renewable energy capacity in India. The agreement has lifted investor interest as rising data centre and AI demand boosts the outlook for clean power.

Sensex up 500 points: Why are markets rising despite fresh US-Iran escalationSensex rose 500 points, while Nifty also gained. The positive move surprised many investors because geopolitical tensions usually trigger a selloff in risk assets.

Markets open higher as investors shrug off US-Iran conflict; RIL, HUL jump 2%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 337.02 points, or 0.46%, to 74,255.78 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 80.35 points, or 0.35%, to trade at 23,322.45 as of 9:27 am.

Meta partners with Reliance for it's first AI data centre in IndiaMeta has partnered with Reliance Industries to lease a 168 MW AI-enabled data centre in Jamnagar. The deal expands their long-running relationship as both companies deepen their AI push in India.

How Trump's crypto empire made $2.3 billion while millions of investors lost bigUS President Donald Trump-linked crypto ventures generated about $2.3 billion for his family. It said more than a million investors lost roughly as much, underscoring the gap between promoter gains and buyer risk.

Standard deduction, NPS and more: 5 tax benefits still available under new regimeThe new tax regime doesn't necessarily close every tax-saving opportunity. Although most popular deductions are no longer available, a few vital benefits are still in place that can help lower your taxable income.

Finance Ministry holds borrowing plans steady, to reassess spending in OctoberSources said the Budget 2026-27 already factored in global uncertainties, giving the government room to handle external shocks.

Sensex, Nifty close in green as easing Israel-Iran tensions lift sentimentThe BSE Sensex gained 394.50 points, or 0.54%, to close at 73,918.76. The NSE Nifty50 rose 119.10 points, or 0.52%, to settle at 23,242.10.

The inspection that hid more than it revealed, and the enforcement reform now neededIn the 10th part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines a case where detailed scrutiny of company records exposed inspection failures, missing disclosures and substantial under-reporting of coverable employees.

From Oman to Gujarat: The mega pipeline that could reshape India's energy futureEvery flare-up in the Strait of Hormuz sends shockwaves through global energy markets. Could a proposed 2,000-km undersea pipeline from Oman to Gujarat help India break free from that vulnerability? Let's find out.

Why the government cut Ujjwala LPG subsidy from nine to four cylinders a yearThe government has said the cut in subsidised Ujjwala LPG refills was driven by alleged diversion of cylinders for commercial use.

OpenAI files confidential IPO, eyes trillion-dollar valuation in AI boomThe company announced on Monday that it had submitted confidential paperwork for a stock market listing. However, OpenAI did not reveal the size of the offering, its expected valuation, or when it plans to go public.

Ujjwala LPG subsidy down to first 4 refills amid conflict-driven crude price surgeEarlier, PMUY beneficiaries received a Rs 300 subsidy on the first nine LPG refills annually. The government reduced this to four refills, citing average household consumption of about four cylinders per year.

India posts Q4 current account surplus as services exports surgeAccording to data released by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Monday, the country's current account recorded a surplus of USD 7.1 billion, or 0.7 per cent of GDP, in the fourth quarter of 2025-26.

Oil, growth, inflation: CEA explains India's key risks amid West Asia crisisSpeaking to India Today, CEA V Anantha Nageswaran said India's economy remains strong, but uncertainty around the ongoing conflict and energy prices continues to pose risks.

entertainment.headline: Dhamaal 4 gets a new release date as Ajay Devgn shares major update Ajay Devgn has shared a fresh update on Dhamaal 4, confirming its theatrical release date and unveiling a new look from the film. Here's what the latest announcement reveals.

Who was Kumod Raney, the friend Salman Khan's family is mourning? Salman Khan was visibly emotional as he attended the funeral of close family friend Kumod Raney in Mumbai on Tuesday. But who was the Dubai-based entrepreneur whose death has left the Khan family mourning?

Alia Bhatt's Alpha teaser draws Ballerina comparisons, fans ask 'where is Sharvari' The teaser for Alpha, starring Alia Bhatt and Bobby Deol, triggered a flood of online reactions, with many viewers praising its action while asking about Sharvari's absence. The response also brought Ballerina comparisons and theories around Bobby Deol's role.

Ram Charan's Peddi injury didn't stop him from completing key scene: Sham Kaushal Veteran action director Sham Kaushal discusses supervising the wrestling sequences in Ram Charan's Peddi, the actor's commitment despite injury, the film's emotional storytelling, and comparisons to Dangal.

Raha's sweet moment with Alia Bhatt at Lara Dhawan's birthday party goes viral A sweet moment between Raha Kapoor and Alia Bhatt at Lara Dhawan's birthday party is winning over fans online. The viral video has sparked reactions and conversations across social media.
Alpha teaser: 'Wolf ki beti' Alia Bhatt strikes like a storm, Bobby Deol joins The first teaser of Alpha is out. Starring Alia Bhatt in a never-seen-before look, it is directed by Shiv Rawail.

Bharathiraja dies at 84: CM Vijay pays tribute, announces full state honour Chief Minister Joseph Vijay visited director Bharathiraja's Chennai home, offered condolences and announced full State honours for the funeral procession. Tributes from film personalities, MK Stalin and Chiranjeevi underscored the director's enduring influence on Tamil cinema.

Hunar Hali and Mayank Gandhi's divorce finalised after nine years of marriage Hunar Hali and Mayank Gandhi were granted a divorce on 9 June after filing for it last September. The decree closed the legal proceedings after settlement issues were resolved and an amicable agreement was reached.

Delhi CM Rekha Gupta reviews Kangana's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata, to make it tax-free Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta praised Kangana Ranaut's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata after a special screening and said she would recommend it be made tax-free in the capital.

Bharathiraja: The director who took Tamil Cinema out of studio and into the soil Bharathiraja, the filmmaker who took Tamil cinema out of the studio and into real villages, died in Chennai on June 10, 2026, after a prolonged illness. His films reshaped the industry's visual and emotional language, making village life central to modern Tamil cinema.

To infinity and beyond: Taylor Swift surprises fans with Toy Story song performance Taylor Swift stunned fans at the Toy Story 5 world premiere in Los Angeles with a special appearance. But it wasn't just a red carpet moment—her involvement with the film took an unexpected turn on stage.

Eva Grover on her troubled marriage to Aamir Khan's step-brother: He was violent Actor Eva Grover said she married Hyder Ali Khan after 18 days of dating and later faced violence in the relationship. She said support from her colleagues, mother and former in-laws helped her leave.

Kusha Kapila, Dolly Singh join outrage against Pranit More over lead outrage Content creators Kusha Kapila and Dolly Singh called out comedian Pranit More over a viral crowd-work video, slamming his handling of a misogynistic audience remark.

Shalin Bhanot reacts to remarriage rumours with Dalljiet Kaur, demands apology Shalin Bhanot has reacted sharply to viral claims about remarrying his former wife Dalljiet Kaur. The actor has issued a warning and demanded immediate action over the reports.

I lost my beautiful sister today: Sohail Khan mourns Kumod Raney's death Sohail Khan mourned the death of longtime family friend Kumod Raney with an emotional social media post. His tribute drew condolences from several celebrities and fans after Raney's death on June 9.

Veteran Tamil director Bharathiraja dies due to prolonged illness at 84 in Chennai Veteran Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja died in Chennai on Monday after prolonged ill health. He was 84. More details regarding the funeral are awaited.

Baahubali to Peddi: How pan-India cinema exposed South cinema's gender problem A scene in Peddi showing Ram Charan kissing Janhvi Kapoor without consent triggered a national backlash. Would Janhvi Kapoor's objectification in a Telugu film have become a national debate if India hadn't embraced the pan-India cinema culture?

Lock Upp Season 2: Is Kangana Ranaut returning as host? Here's what we know Lock Upp is making a comeback after four years with a new OTT home and a fresh season. But will Kangana Ranaut return as host? Here's everything revealed so far about the release date, streaming platform and hosting rumours.

Katy Perry goes full heart-eyes for Justin Trudeau, calls him the 'love of my life' Katy Perry isn't holding back anymore. The singer publicly called Justin Trudeau the "love of my life" at her Tribeca premiere, revealing how the former Canadian Prime Minister makes her feel "whole" after a tumultuous year.

general news.headline: Second blow for Mamata; TMC's Sushmita Dev quits as Rajya Sabha MP, may join BJPIn a surprise move, Sushmita Dev met Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma in Delhi after resigning from the Rajya Sabha, with sources telling India Today that she is likely to join the BJP.

Unlawful: Congress urges EC to reverse Meenakshi Natarajan nomination rejectionA high-stakes legal battle has reached the Election Commission's doorstep. Congress claims its Rajya Sabha candidate was denied a level playing field and wants immediate intervention before the withdrawal deadline expires.

POK on edge, Zojila cheer in India: The tale of two Kashmirs divided by LoCSecurity forces in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (POK) killed at least 27 people even as they did everything possible to foil a protest. Some 100 km to the east, engineers in Jammu and Kashmir celebrated the Zojila Tunnel breakthrough. The two developments show the divergent trajectories taken by two Kashmirs divided by the Line of Control (LoC).

What Modi said on becoming India's longest-serving PMHaving first assumed office on May 26, 2014, Narendra Modi completed 4,399 consecutive days as Prime Minister, overtaking the record of India's first Prime Minister, Jawaharlal Nehru, who served 4,398 uninterrupted days as the head of an elected government after the first general elections in 1952.

The Opposition Doesn't Need INDIA 2.0. It Needs Congress 2.0Without a deeper Congress-led political reunification, INDIA 2.0 risks becoming less a revival of the opposition and more a sequel struggling to recreate an old hit.

Live: Abhishek Banerjee, Rahul Gandhi discuss Bengal situation in 90-minute meetingTMC Crisis Live Updates: Troubles continue to mount for Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee. Amid a raging rebellion within the party, the West Bengal CID on Tuesday conducted simultaneous searches at the TMC's central office at her Kalighat residence and at national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee's Camac Street office in Kolkata, as part of its probe into alleged forged signatures of TMC MLAs that triggered the party split. The agency officers maintained that the raids were held to dredge out the original copy of TMC's meeting resolution book and attendance sheet containing signatures of 70 MLAs present at the May 6 meeting held the party office adjacent to former chief minister Mamata Banerjee's residence. Both Mamata Banerjee and Abhishek were out of town, at the national capital for their scheduled meetings with leaders of the opposition INDIA bloc. Follow this space for all the live updates

Godzilla El Nino coming: From farms to power grids, nothing will be sparedA climate shift is taking shape far from India, but it could eventually reach homes, farms, and businesses across the country. Here's why scientists are paying close attention.

Sushmita Dev gone too: The two factors driving exodus of TMC MPsTrinamool Congress MPs are aware of the massive backlash that local party leaders are facing in West Bengal. Public fury, erosion of support and an enabling BJP is reportedly making TMC MPs from the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha ditch Mamata Banerjee and side with the NDA. The latest being Sushmita Dev.

The men beside me during Peddi explain why Janhvi is hyper-sexualised in the filmI watched Ram Charan's Peddi alone, and the two men seated beside me kept laughing through Janhvi Kapoor's scenes while repeatedly invading my personal space. If this is how some people react in theatres, perhaps it's time we questioned what we're normalising on screen.

CM talks about women safety, reality differs: Court after Vijay govt's report mix-upThe Madras High Court questioned authorities after a hospital submitted an abortion fitness report instead of the medical details it had sought. The mix-up prompted Justice Victoria Gowri to raise concerns over the enquiry process and women's safety claims in the state.

African asylum seeker tries to 'behead' man in Belfast, anti-immigration stir eruptsAuthorities have charged a 30-year-old Sudanese asylum seeker with attempted murder after the attack, which left the man fighting for his life and ignited public anger that spilled onto the streets.

Isro's LVM3 that launched Chandrayaan-3 to be handed to private sectorIN-SPACe has invited Indian companies to take over the end-to-end realisation, operation and commercialisation of Isro's LVM3 rocket.
Alpha teaser: 'Wolf ki beti' Alia Bhatt strikes like a storm, Bobby Deol joinsThe first teaser of Alpha is out. Starring Alia Bhatt in a never-seen-before look, it is directed by Shiv Rawail.

Please help quickly, ship sinking: Indian crew's SOS after US missile strikeA US strike hit the tanker Marivex off Oman, prompting distress calls from its 24 Indian crew as fire broke out on board. All sailors were rescued by Oman, while the attack drew union criticism amid rising Gulf shipping tensions.

Trump's $100,000 H-1B plan is dead. Indians are breathing easierA US federal court has struck down Donald Trump's proposed $100,000 H-1B visa fee, offering major relief to Indian students, professionals and employers. The ruling preserves a crucial pathway from education to employment in America and restores confidence in a system that many feared was becoming unpredictable.

Madhya Pradesh woman flaunts gold jewellery, cash on YouTube; thieves rob her laterA burglary at Rachna Gurjar's Shivpuri home is being probed for a possible social media link. Police are examining CCTV footage and whether online posts helped the accused choose the house.

Bankipur bypoll emerges as Prashant Kishor's biggest political testIf Jan Suraaj performs strongly in Bankipur, a long-time BJP bastion represented by Nitin Nabin, Prashant Kishor could project it as evidence that his politics resonates with urban and aspirational voters. A poor showing, however, would intensify doubts over both Jan Suraaj's future and Kishor's political experiment in Bihar.

Can E85 flex-fuel vehicles run on E20 fuel safely? Here is the answerWith the advent of E85 flex-fuel technology, the internal combustion engine is making a significant move toward a greener future. But can an E85 flex-fuel vehicle operate safely on E20 fuel? Here's what you need to know.

The Supreme Court has reset the moral compass on pre-marital sexThe Supreme Court said a consensual relationship between unmarried adults cannot by itself define character. The observation shifts the conversation from moral judgment to consent, autonomy and personal choice.

Married and anonymous: What 4.5 crore+ Indians seek on 'secret' dating appsMore than 4.5 crore Indians have used discreet dating apps catering to anonymous encounters, open relationships and affairs. A closer look reveals users' motivations, evolving relationship trends and the business of monetising desire.

Gold falls below Rs 1.5 lakh, silver drops Rs 4,000. Here's whyGold and silver slumped in India after heavy selling in global bullion markets. A stronger dollar, rising oil prices and revived rate hike fears deepened pressure on precious metals.

This Indian city made it to the world's best food cities list. It's not Delhi or LucknowAn Indian city down south has featured on Time Out's World's Best Food Cities 2026 ranking. Thanks to its aromatic biryani, creative cocktail bars and vibrant culinary scene.

Iran claims US strikes hit 2 reservoirs, coastal town left without drinking waterTehran alleged that US strikes disrupted essential services in Sirik, with local authorities reporting a loss of drinking water access. The Iranian Consulate in Mumbai condemned the attack and called it a breach of humanitarian norms.

sports.headline: Somalia gives hero's welcome to FIFA World Cup referee denied entry by US: Video Somalia gave Omar Abdulkadir Artan a hero's welcome after US authorities denied him entry for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The award-winning referee landed in the capital city of Mogadishu on Wednesday, June 10.

Curacao to Cape Verde: The unmissable minnows at FIFA World Cup 2026 From Curacao's historic debut to long-awaited returns for Haiti and DR Congo, alongside Cape Verde's tactical brilliance, the expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup gives football's ultimate minnows a shot at global immortality. Here is all that you need to know about them.

Camera comes crashing down in football match, crew narrowly escapes huge mishap While the FIFA World Cup has been muddled with on and off-field controversies, things are not better for the federations outside of the global tournament as well. The latest disaster has taken place in a friendly between Hungary and Kazakhstan, where a spider-cam suspended with wires above the stadium, came crashing down on the pitch.

Asian Games: No Babar, Shaheen as Sahibzada Farhan to lead Pakistan's young squad Pakistan have handed Sahibzada Farhan the captaincy of a youthful squad for the 2026 Asian Games, leaving out several established white-ball stars. The selection signals a shift in focus following recent tournament disappointments.

England can dare to dream but are not heavy favourites, says Thomas Tuchel England manager Thomas Tuchel believes his side have the quality to challenge for the World Cup title, but insists they cannot be considered favourites given their long wait for success on football's biggest stage.

After Virat Kohli, Hardik Pandya ruled out of ODI series vs Afghanistan After Virat Kohli, Hardik Pandya has been ruled out of India's ruled out of the 3-match ODI series vs Afghanistan. BCCI sources told India Today that Hardik is suffering a quadricep injury which will take three weeks to heal.

Lionel Messi scores on comeback as Argentina cruise past Iceland 3-0 Lionel Messi marked his return from injury with a goal as Argentina completed their FIFA World Cup preparations in style, beating Iceland 3-0 in a friendly at Auburn, Alabama, on Tuesday.

Writing exams and beating Full Members? Netherlands women eye more at T20 World Cup The Netherlands head into their maiden Women's T20 World Cup believing they can compete with the sport's biggest teams after a historic win over Bangladesh. For a squad balancing studies, jobs, and cricket, the tournament is also a chance to earn franchise opportunities and keep their cricketing dreams alive.

World Championship a burden? Most people lucky not to achieve it, says D Gukesh Bleeding rating points and fresh off a bruising Norway Chess campaign, World Champion D Gukesh opens up about the devastating psychological toll of climbing the peak too early.

Can India Women end T20 World Cup heartbreak after ODI glory? ICC Women's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur guided India to their maiden ICC title last year. Now, the Indian skipper will be hoping to carry that winning momentum into the Women's T20 World Cup, a trophy that has so far eluded India despite several near misses on the global stage.

Inglis disappointed as Australia suffer first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years Bangladesh beat Australia by 86 runs in the first ODI in Dhaka, with Josh Inglis left frustrated by a flat team performance. The defeat ended Australia's 21-year unbeaten run against Bangladesh in ODIs and handed the hosts a 1-0 lead in the three-match series.

Atletico ridicule Real Madrid after rejecting blockbuster offer for Julian AlvarezReal Madrid's attempt to sign Julian Alvarez for a club-record €150 million was met with ridicule from Atletico Madrid, who swiftly rejected the offer and publicly taunted their city rivals.

Serena Williams mulls Wimbledon comeback after winning return to tennisSerena Williams has not ruled out a return to Wimbledon after making a winning comeback to professional tennis nearly four years after her last competitive match.

FIFA World Cup 2026 wonder kids: 5 breakout stars set to explode transfer marketFIFA World Cup 2026 breakout stars: From Nico Paz's pristine vision to Endrick's clinical scoring aura, a new generation of fearless prodigies is ready to take centre stage in North America.

Pooja Vastrakar reveals how RCB helped her regain confidenceIndia all-rounder Pooja Vastrakar has credited Royal Challengers Bengaluru for backing her through a difficult injury phase, saying the franchise supported her recovery and focused on her batting while easing her back into bowling.

Alexander Zverev abruptly cuts off interview after domestic violence questionsAlexander Zverev finally won his first Grand Slam title at the 2026 French Open, defeating Flavio Cobolli. However, his achievement was quickly overshadowed as post-match discussions shifted to past domestic violence allegations.

England captain Ben Stokes could retire after nightclub controversyBen Stokes' England future has been thrown into doubt after a nightclub controversy triggered an ECB investigation. Reports suggest the Test captain could lose the captaincy, take a break from cricket, or even retire from international cricket altogether.

From Alana King to Nensi Patel: Indian-origin stars to watch out forIndia head into the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 as one of the title contenders after their ODI World Cup triumph. But beyond Harmanpreet Kaur's side, several Indian-origin players representing other nations are also set to leave their mark.

A World Cup of chaos: Ex-England star blasts hosts USA over visa messIan Wright has labelled the FIFA World Cup 2026 the "World Cup of chaos" amid mounting concerns over visa denials and travel disruptions. The former England star's criticism follows the exclusion of African referee Omar Artan from the tournament.

Pakistan endure Women's T20 World Cup warm-up horror after shock defeat to ScotlandPakistan's preparations for the Women's T20 World Cup suffered another setback as they slumped to a defeat against Scotland. Pakistan's batting faltered, leaving Fatima Sana's side with plenty to address ahead of a daunting group-stage campaign.

Gaikwad proves selectors wrong as India-A win thriller in DambullaIndia A beat Sri Lanka A in an absolute thriller in Dambulla on Tuesday, June 9. Playing their first match in the tri-series, India dragged the match back in the final 3 overs to win by 8 runs.

Indian football crisis nears its end as AIFF and ISL teams agree on club-led modelThe Indian Super League is set to move forward after the AIFF agreed in principle to a two-year club-led model. The breakthrough, backed by the Sports Ministry, clears the path for the 2026-27 season to begin on September 4.

Group B preview: Can West Indies, Sri Lanka surprise the favourites?Women's T20 World Cup: England and New Zealand are widely considered favourites to reach the semifinals, but West Indies and Sri Lanka have the ability to spring surprises. Meanwhile, Ireland and Scotland will be looking to cause a few upsets along the way.

Arjun Tendulkar shines in Mumbai T20 League, gets big dressing room ovationArjun Tendulkar produced a stunning all-round display to power Arcs Andheri to a dominant win over Bandra Blasters in the T20 Mumbai League. The youngster was later rewarded with an emotional dressing-room ovation led by captain Shivam Dube.

Workhorse Mohammed Siraj withdrawn from India's T20I tour of England and IrelandMohammed Siraj has been rested from India's T20Is in Ireland and England under a workload plan. Prasidh Krishna replaces him as India manage resources before a long white-ball season, with Siraj retained for the ODI leg.

FIFA denies tickets to Iran football fans just days ahead of World CupIran's football federation has accused FIFA of pulling its World Cup ticket allocation just days before the tournament begins, leaving thousands of supporters in limbo after many had already booked flights, accommodation and travel plans to follow the national team.

Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya cleared fit for ODIs vs AfghanistanIND vs AFG: Hardik Pandya and Rohit Sharma have been cleared for India's ODI series against Afghanistan. Their return strengthens the batting-bowling balance, while Virat Kohli remains currently sidelined with a hamstring injury.

Footballer detained, referee deported: FIFA World Cup is a mess before kick-offJust days before the beginning of football's greatest spectacle, the FIFA World Cup has run into a myriad of controversies. The latest of those involves Africa's best football referee.

Bangladesh T20 captain reacts to WC boycott: Can play in Pak, then why not India?Litton Das has challenged Bangladesh's official line on pulling out of the T20 World Cup in India over security. His account raises fresh questions about why the call was eventually made.

SL A vs IND A highlights: India beat Sri Lanka by 8 runs in Dambulla thrillerSri Lanka A vs India A highlights: Sri Lanka-A saw their final three wickets go down in the 49th over, as the Ruturaj Gaikwad-led India snatched away an 8-run win over the hosts in some formidable clutch style. 2 wickets and a run out in Arshad Khan's penultimate over marked the end of a thrilling clash in Dambulla, which did not even have a Vaibhav Sooryavanshi show.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
